metadata.description: Gen Z / Gen Alpha dream-adjacent vocabulary, bilingual EN/ZH, mapped to interpretive and generative contexts. Sources: TikTok (EN/zh), X/Twitter, Discord, Reddit, 小红书, 豆瓣, Lofter. Not a dictionary — a register guide. The purpose is to know when to use a term, when to echo it back, and when to silently translate it into interpretive craft.
metadata.ai_application: When a user brings a term from this list, do not define it back at them. Use it naturally in the response. When generating dream interpretations or captions, select from this register based on platform and inferred age. Never force slang the user hasn't used.
metadata.currency_warning: Slang ages fast. Anything marked 'peak' may have decayed within 6 months. Re-audit quarterly.

emotional_register.usage: Affirming the dream was intense and executed hard. 'My subconscious ate that one.'
emotional_register.dream_context: Best for surreal dreams that landed emotionally.

emotional_register.usage: For vague, contentless dreams. 'No thoughts, head empty, just vibes.'

emotional_register.zh_equivalent: 感觉不一样 / 后劲大
emotional_register.usage: A dream that carries unusual emotional weight compared to a normal one.
Something recurring in dreams without invitation. 'He's been living rent free in my dreams for 3 weeks.'

emotional_register.zh_equivalent: 核心记忆 / 人生高光
emotional_register.usage: Inside Out reference. A dream that feels formative. 'Core memory unlocked.'
Dream delivers narrative backstory as if the subconscious were world-building.
'My dream gave this random stranger a full backstory' — impressed at the subconscious's worldbuilding.
Dream characters that feel non-sentient. 'The people in the dream were total NPCs.'
emotional_register.dream_context: Useful for describing dream-character quality and testing lucidity.

emotional_register.usage: A dream sub-plot that doesn't resolve the main dream. 'Dream gave me a 20-minute side quest about my dentist.'
'I was the main character of my dream' — self-insertion with agency.
Dream compressed decades. Classic dream-time-distortion trope, now meme-ified.

intensity_and_weirdness.zh_equivalent: 发烧梦 / 离谱到不真实
intensity_and_weirdness.usage: Now used for anything surreal or chaotic — does not require actual fever. 'That whole night was a fever dream.'
intensity_and_weirdness.cross_platform: Universal EN + zh equivalents in use

intensity_and_weirdness.zh_equivalent: 脑腐梦 / 脑子坏掉的梦
intensity_and_weirdness.usage: Dream dominated by whatever algorithm you've been consuming — TikTok slop, specific meme, parasocial fixation.
intensity_and_weirdness.interpretation_hint: Worth asking: what were you scrolling before bed? Brain rot dreams are often algorithmic residue.

intensity_and_weirdness.usage: Dream had no rules, no logic, felt psychotic. 'Unhinged dream, 10/10, would repress again.'

intensity_and_weirdness.zh_equivalent: 剧情崩了 / 人设崩了
intensity_and_weirdness.usage: Dream started coherent, collapsed into incoherence.
'That was a very Capricorn-coded dream.' / 'Very AuDHD-coded.' Uses identity markers to taxonomize dream content.
intensity_and_weirdness.interpretation_hint: The user is doing symbolic interpretation — take their frame seriously.

intensity_and_weirdness.usage: 'My dream's weird flex was that I could speak fluent Russian.'
Dream refused to be dramatic. 'Very unserious dream, don't analyze it.'

romantic_and_parasocial.usage: Self-aware delusion. 'Delulu dream where my crush confessed.' K-pop stan-culture origin.
romantic_and_parasocial.tone: always self-aware, never pathologizing — if AI uses it, it must also be self-aware

romantic_and_parasocial.usage: 'I'm manifesting that dream into reality.' Dreams as pre-cognition/visualization tools.
romantic_and_parasocial.interpretation_hint: User may be in a manifesting/spiritual frame. Don't debunk, don't endorse — mirror.

romantic_and_parasocial.usage: Reality-shifting community (see shifting_subculture.json). 'I shifted to my DR last night.' Not metaphor — they mean it.
Justifying dream choices that would be weird awake.
'My partner dream-cheated on me and I'm still mad.' Extremely common recurring X discourse.
'Haunted by a dream I had in 7th grade.' Long-tail memory.
'My subconscious soft-launched him before I noticed IRL.'
Dream revealed a turn-off you didn't know you had. 'Ick dream: he chewed loud.' Dating-app era phenomenon.

spiritual_and_shadow.zh_equivalent: 阴影功课 / 原生家庭修复
spiritual_and_shadow.usage: Jungian term absorbed into TikTok wellness discourse. 'Dream was pure shadow work.'
spiritual_and_shadow.interpretation_hint: If user brings this, they are in a Jungian frame. Honor it — don't over-academic, don't dismiss as TikTok pop psych.

spiritual_and_shadow.usage: 'My inner child showed up in the dream.' Extremely common in Chinese and English therapy-adjacent discourse.
'The dream felt like my higher self speaking.'
Dream as transmission. 'I downloaded so much in that dream.'
'That dream was a portal.' Both literal (dream imagery) and metaphorical (life transition).
'Dream was a sign.' Frame-mirror: don't confirm or deny, ask what sign they felt.

meta_and_platform.zh_equivalent: POV 梦 / 第一视角梦
meta_and_platform.usage: TikTok caption convention spilled into dream description. 'POV: dream where you're late to an exam forever.'
'The dream felt like a simulation glitch.' Simulation-theory-literate frame.
'Dream glitched when I tried to read a sign.' Classic reality-check artifact reframed as glitch.
Dreams experienced as having been delivered by the algorithm of the subconscious.
Meta-commentary on dream content as a kind of currency — shared, rated, consumed.
Scripted-feeling speech from dream characters. 'Dream NPC said the weirdest thing.'

darker_register.usage: Originally anthropology (van Gennep, Turner). Now used for any eerily empty transitional space. 'That dream was liminal as hell.'
Freud's unheimlich, filtered through horror discourse. 'Uncanny dream — my house but not my house.'
Minimal, evocative. 'Dream felt wrong. Nothing specific.' Good signal for pre-traumatic or anxiety content.
darker_register.interpretation_hint: Ask gently what felt wrong rather than forcing specifics.

darker_register.usage: 'Something was off about the house / her / the sky.'
Pre-event fear without object. Distinct from fear. Often pre-dream-climax.
Soft-spoken way of saying ominous. Common Gen Z de-escalation of horror vocabulary.

zh_native_slang_without_en_equivalent.usage: Wholesale emotional collapse, often temporary. '醒来 emo 了一整天.'
zh_native_slang_without_en_equivalent.note: Borrowed English 'emo' now functions as intransitive verb in Chinese.

zh_native_slang_without_en_equivalent.usage: Emotionally undone by something unexpectedly moving. '那个梦直接把我破防了.'
Intense, can't stop thinking about it. '这梦太上头了.'
Affect persists long after waking. '这梦后劲真大.'
Frustrated by dream content. '梦里还在加班,血压直接上来了.'
Absurd. Catch-all for dream weirdness. '离谱到离谱他妈给离谱开门.'
Sarcastic thanks, resigned. '做了这种梦,栓 Q 了.'
zh_native_slang_without_en_equivalent.status: 2022-2024 peak, now slightly cringe — use sparingly

ai_application_playbook.register_matching: Match the user's register tightly. If they wrote '鬼压床 emo 了', don't reply in clinical Chinese. If they wrote 'unhinged fever dream', don't reply in academic English.
ai_application_playbook.avoid_over_slang: Using 3+ pieces of slang in a single response reads as trying-too-hard. One well-placed term per paragraph maximum.
ai_application_playbook.cultural_translation: Do not mechanically translate EN slang to ZH or vice versa when user has used one side. They know the other side exists. 'delulu' and '妄想' are not identical — delulu is self-aware and affectionate, 妄想 is clinical.

generation_task_fit.dream_interpretation: Use 3-5 terms total, load them in the framing, not the analysis itself.
generation_task_fit.share_card_caption: Use 1-3 terms, depending on platform (see douyin_xiaohongshu_bili.json).
generation_task_fit.video_prompt: Do NOT feed slang into image/video model prompts — translate into visual descriptors.
generation_task_fit.dm_reply_to_user: Match their slang density exactly. No more, no less.

never_do: Define a slang term the user just used. (Condescending.)
Invent slang. Use only what's listed here or what the user brings.
Use 'based' or 'cringe' in interpretive output — those carry value judgments unsuitable for the AI's role.
